evals.name: version-constraint-1.21
evals.trap: The project targets Go 1.21. Features like range-over-int (1.22), loop variable fix (1.22), cmp.Or (1.22), and math/rand/v2 (1.22) are NOT available. The model must only suggest 1.21-compatible changes AND must avoid false positives by staying within version constraints.

assertions.text: Suggests min/max builtins to replace minInt/maxInt (available in Go 1.21)
Suggests slices.Sort or slices.Contains (available in Go 1.21)
Suggests sync.OnceValue to replace manual sync.Once pattern (available in Go 1.21)
Does NOT suggest range-over-int for processN (requires Go 1.22+)
Does NOT suggest removing loop variable shadow copy v := v in startWorkers (requires Go 1.22+)
Does NOT suggest math/rand/v2 migration (requires Go 1.22+)
Does NOT suggest cmp.Or (requires Go 1.22+)

evals.name: rand-v2-api-renames
evals.trap: math/rand/v2 renames functions: Intn->IntN, Int63n->Int64N. Read is removed entirely. Seed is unnecessary. Without the skill, the model may keep old function names.

assertions.text: Renames Intn to IntN (capital N)
Renames Int63n to Int64N (not Int63N or Int64n)
Removes all rand.Seed calls (automatic seeding in v2)
Replaces rand.Read with crypto/rand usage for random bytes
Import changes to math/rand/v2
Does NOT keep any old-style function names (no rand.Intn or rand.Int63n in output)

evals.name: safety-over-cosmetic
evals.trap: The code has a path traversal vulnerability (filepath.Join + filepath.Clean is insufficient). Without the skill, the model may prioritize cosmetic changes (interface{}->any, min builtin) over the security-critical os.Root fix.

assertions.text: Suggests os.Root/os.OpenRoot for user-supplied file paths
Mentions path traversal risk, directory escape, or CWE-22
Prioritizes the safety fix (os.Root) over cosmetic changes
Also suggests interface{} -> any
Also suggests min builtin or net.JoinHostPort
Does NOT only address cosmetic issues without mentioning the security issue

evals.name: omitzero-vs-omitempty
evals.prompt:
Review these JSON struct tags for correctness in our Go 1.24 API. Users report that zero-value time fields and false booleans are unexpectedly included or omitted in JSON responses.

```go
package api

import "time"

type Event struct {
    ID        string    `json:"id"`
    Name      string    `json:"name"`
    StartAt   time.Time `json:"start_at,omitempty"`
    EndAt     time.Time `json:"end_at,omitempty"`
    Cancelled bool      `json:"cancelled,omitempty"`
    Archived  bool      `json:"archived,omitempty"`
    Notes     string    `json:"notes,omitempty"`
    Priority  int       `json:"priority,omitempty"`
}

type UserSettings struct {
    UserID           string    `json:"user_id"`
    DarkMode         bool      `json:"dark_mode,omitempty"`
    EmailNotifs      bool      `json:"email_notifs,omitempty"`
    LastLogin        time.Time `json:"last_login,omitempty"`
    AccountCreated   time.Time `json:"account_created,omitempty"`
    Bio              string    `json:"bio,omitempty"`
}
```

Explain the issue and provide the corrected code.
evals.trap: omitempty doesn't work correctly for time.Time (zero time is a non-empty struct) and treats false as empty for bool. Go 1.24 introduced omitzero which handles both correctly. Without the skill, the model may not know about omitzero.

assertions.text: Identifies that omitempty doesn't omit zero time.Time (it's a non-empty struct)
Suggests omitzero for time.Time fields (StartAt, EndAt, LastLogin, AccountCreated)
Identifies that omitempty treats false as empty for bool fields
Addresses bool issue correctly (removes tag or uses omitzero)
Correctly notes omitzero requires Go 1.24+
Does NOT suggest omitzero for string or int fields where omitempty works correctly

evals.name: benchmark-b-loop
evals.trap: Go 1.24 introduced b.Loop() which replaces the manual for i := 0; i < b.N; i++ pattern. Without the skill, the model likely doesn't know about b.Loop().

assertions.text: Replaces for i := 0; i < b.N; i++ with for b.Loop() in BenchmarkMarshal
Replaces for n := 0; n < b.N; n++ with for b.Loop() in BenchmarkUnmarshal
Replaces the b.N loop in BenchmarkRoundTrip too
Does NOT keep any b.N iteration pattern in the output
Preserves benchmark function names and logic

evals.name: automaxprocs-removal
evals.trap: Go 1.25 has built-in container-aware GOMAXPROCS, making uber-go/automaxprocs unnecessary. The model must know this Go 1.25 addition — without the skill it will likely treat automaxprocs as still needed and only suggest sync.WaitGroup.Go.

assertions.text: Suggests removing go.uber.org/automaxprocs import and dependency
Explains that Go 1.25 has built-in container-aware GOMAXPROCS (or cgroup CPU limits awareness)
Suggests sync.WaitGroup.Go to replace Add/go func/Done pattern
Does NOT suggest keeping the automaxprocs dependency as still necessary
Suggests running go mod tidy to remove the dependency from go.sum

evals.name: cmp-or-chained-defaults
evals.trap: Go 1.22 introduced cmp.Or(a, b, c...) which returns the first non-zero value. It collapses multi-step default chains to single lines. Without the skill, the model will likely keep the if/else chains or use a custom helper function.

assertions.text: Uses cmp.Or for at least one default value chain
Collapses the 3-step host default to a single cmp.Or call
Import includes the cmp package
All multi-step defaults converted to cmp.Or (host, port, logLevel, region)
Result is functionally equivalent (same fallback order)
Does NOT introduce a custom helper function for defaults

evals.name: addcleanup-vs-setfinalizer
evals.trap: runtime.SetFinalizer is deprecated since Go 1.24 in favor of runtime.AddCleanup. The key API difference: AddCleanup takes the resource as a separate argument (not the whole object). Without the skill the model is unlikely to know AddCleanup exists or that SetFinalizer's cycle restriction is the main problem.

assertions.text: Replaces runtime.SetFinalizer with runtime.AddCleanup
AddCleanup cleanup function receives the resource (db/*sql.DB or fd/int) as a separate argument, NOT the whole wrapper struct
Explains that SetFinalizer prevents GC when the object holds a reference to itself or another object with a finalizer (cycle restriction)
Does NOT pass ManagedConn or TempFile directly as the resource to AddCleanup
Correctly attributes runtime.AddCleanup to Go 1.24 (or notes it is the modern replacement)

evals.name: http-mux-migration
evals.trap: Go 1.22 added method+path pattern routing to net/http. The exact syntax is 'METHOD /path/{param}' as the pattern string. mux.Vars(r) becomes r.PathValue('id'). Without the skill, the model might not use the correct syntax or might suggest a different third-party router.

assertions.text: Uses http.NewServeMux() instead of mux.NewRouter()
Uses method prefix in patterns like 'GET /api/users/{id}'
Uses r.PathValue("id") instead of mux.Vars(r)
All 6 routes migrated with correct method prefixes (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE)
No gorilla/mux import remains
Return type changes from *mux.Router to *http.ServeMux

evals.name: synctest-flaky-fix
evals.trap: Go 1.25 introduced testing/synctest.Test which provides deterministic concurrent testing with synctest.Wait(). The natural fix is to increase sleep, use channels for sync, or add retries. The skill teaches synctest.Test as the modern solution. Note: synctest.Run is deprecated since Go 1.25 — must use synctest.Test.

assertions.text: Uses synctest.Test (NOT synctest.Run which is deprecated since Go 1.25)
Uses synctest.Wait() for goroutine synchronization
Removes all time.Sleep calls
No flaky timing dependencies remain
Correctly imports testing/synctest
Both tests converted (TestBrokerPubSub and TestBrokerMultiTopic)

evals.name: waitgroup-go-loopvar
evals.trap: Go 1.25 introduced sync.WaitGroup.Go. Go 1.22+ fixed loop variable semantics (v := v copies are unnecessary). Go 1.24+ has t.Context(). Without the skill, the model may not know WaitGroup.Go exists and may keep the shadow copies.

assertions.text: Replaces Add/go func/Done pattern with wg.Go(func() { ... })
Removes wg.Add(1) calls
Removes defer wg.Done() calls
Removes item := item loop variable shadow copies
Explains Go 1.22+ loop variable semantics make copies unnecessary
Replaces context.Background() with t.Context() in test
Preserves WaitGroup.Wait() call

evals.name: timer-gc-greenteagc
evals.trap: Go 1.23+ timers/tickers are GC'd without Stop(). The Stop() in PeriodicTask is still needed for correctness. Go 1.26's Green Tea GC (10-40% overhead reduction) may make manual tuning unnecessary. go fix ./... is available in Go 1.26.

assertions.text: Identifies that some defer timer.Stop() calls are unnecessary with Go 1.23+
Explains the timer/ticker GC behavior change (collected without Stop)
Suggests reviewing GC tuning (SetGCPercent/SetMemoryLimit) due to Green Tea GC
Mentions Green Tea GC's 10-40% overhead reduction
Does NOT suggest removing Stop() from PeriodicTask ticker (needed for correctness)
Suggests go fix ./... for automated modernization (Go 1.26)
Correctly distinguishes between Stop for GC (removable) and Stop for correctness (keep)

evals.name: go126-errors-astype-enhanced-new
evals.trap: Go 1.26 introduced errors.AsType[T]() which replaces the verbose var+errors.As pattern with a single-line if. Go 1.26 also enhanced new() to accept an initial value, replacing the ptr[T] helper. Without the skill, the model likely won't know either feature exists.

assertions.text: Uses errors.AsType[*os.PathError](err) or similar generic form instead of var+errors.As
Replaces the ptr[T] helper function with new() that accepts an initial value (e.g., new(30 * time.Second))
